target_mechanism: HOVER-ATTN: constant-rank pressure pad operator inserted between QK and softmax = essentially Linformer-style low-rank intermediate eliminating O(n^2)

results.title: Hovercraft — Wikipedia
results.note: Source-domain page (hovercraft); not an LLM/attention method.

results.title: Breaking Low-Rank Dilemma of Linear Attention RALA (CVPR 2025)
results.note: Rank-augmented linear attention directly addresses low-rank intermediate to break O(n^2); strong functional overlap with the constant-rank pad framing.

results.title: Contextual Priority Attention (Nature Sci Reports 2025/12)
results.note: Global-context priority achieving O(n log n)/linear; same goal of avoiding O(n^2) via a compressed intermediate.

results.title: A3 Analytical Low-Rank Approximation (2025/5)
results.note: Explicit QK low-rank approximation; directly matches the constant-rank pad-between-QK-and-softmax operator.

results.title: Linformer: Self-Attention with Linear Complexity (2020/6)
results.note: Canonical low-rank projection of K and V to constant rank; HOVER-ATTN is essentially a re-framing of this exact mechanism.

results.title: Performer: Rethinking Attention with FAVOR+ kernel (2020/9)
results.note: Kernel feature-map approximation removes explicit O(n^2) softmax; same end of linear-complexity attention via intermediate factorization.

results.title: Transformer Linear Attention with Optimized GPU Kernel (2025/10)
results.note: Linear attention with kernel implementation; same O(n) goal via compressed intermediate.

results.title: Error-Free Linear Attention is a Free Lunch (2025/12)
results.note: Exact-solution linear attention; same functional class of replacing O(n^2) softmax via intermediate operator.

summary.novelty_assessment: HOVER-ATTN is functionally equivalent to Linformer-style low-rank attention (rank 5 func 0.93); rebadging the constant-rank intermediate as a 'pressure pad' does not add a new mechanism. Strong prior art includes Linformer, A3, RALA, Performer, CPA, and 2025 linear-attention variants. Novelty claim is essentially zero on the LLM side.
